hotels.name: TRUNK Hotel Yoyogi Park
hotels.notes: Shortlist ŌĆö design boutique. Replace once booked.

activities.title: Haneda Airport (HND)
activities.description: Clear customs, grab Suica card, train or taxi to hotel.

activities.title: Afuri Harajuku
activities.description: Yuzu shio ramen ŌĆö Reddit's near-universal pick for a perfect first-night Tokyo meal: light, clean, citrus-forward. ~5 min walk from the hotel. Order from the ticket machine inside; English menu available.

activities.title: Senso-ji Temple
activities.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, 7th century. Walk under Kaminarimon lantern. Early arrival = fewer tour buses.

activities.title: Nakamise-dori snacks
activities.description: Ningyo-yaki, agemanju, melon pan from approach-street stalls. Map opens at Asakusa Kibidango Azuma ŌĆö Street View to scout nearby vendors.

activities.title: Yanaka Ginza
activities.description: Old Tokyo shopping street, cat-themed shops. Map opens at Yuyake Dandan stairs ŌĆö Street View to walk the street.
activities.address: Yuyake Dandan stairs, 3 Chome Yanaka, Taito

activities.title: Omoide Yokocho
activities.description: Smoky yakitori alley by Shinjuku JR tracks. Order momo, seseri, sunagimo.

activities.title: Golden Gai
activities.description: 200+ tiny bars. Try Albatross or Cremaster.

activities.title: Koenji Station
activities.description: 15 min on the Chuo Line from Shimokita.

activities.title: Nintendo Tokyo (Shibuya Parco 6F)
activities.description: Pick up a beautiful hanafuda deck (Nintendo originally made them in 1889 before they made video games). Decks ┬ź800ŌĆō4000. Plan: learn Koi-Koi from a YouTube tutorial during ryokan downtime + play over kaiseki at Ubuya.

activities.title: Kabukiza Theatre
activities.description: Single-act ticket (makumi-ken) at window. Audio guide ┬ź700.

activities.title: Yoyogi Park walk
activities.description: Walk straight from TRUNK Yoyogi into the park. Quiet Thursday morning ŌĆö cedars, joggers, autumn leaves.

activities.title: Meiji Jingu Shrine
activities.description: Major Shinto shrine in a vast forested grove ŌĆö the counterpart to Senso-ji's Buddhist scale. Quiet at this hour, you'll have the gravel paths almost to yourselves. Allow ~90 min round trip from main torii.

activities.title: Uogashi Nihon-Ichi (Shinjuku)
activities.description: Standing sushi bar near Shinjuku South Exit. ┬ź1500-3000 pp, order piece by piece, fresh nigiri at counter pace. Perfect quick pre-train sushi. ~30 min, then 10 min walk to Shinjuku Station.
activities.address: Uogashi Nihon-Ichi Shinjuku Minamiguchi, 3-35-7 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City

activities.title: Japanese breakfast
activities.description: Grilled fish, miso soup, tamagoyaki, rice.

activities.title: Lake Kawaguchi north shore walk
activities.description: Quiet shoreline, Fuji reflections if calm.

activities.title: Kaiseki dinner #2 ┬Ę hanafuda night
activities.description: Multi-course dinner at the ryokan. Bring the Nintendo hanafuda deck picked up Day 3 and learn Koi-Koi over sake ŌĆö watch a quick rules video on the way to dinner. Quiet lake, paper screens, two-player game.

activities.title: Chureito Pagoda sunrise
activities.description: Sunrise mission. Taxi ~20 min from the ryokan. The iconic pagoda-with-Fuji shot ŌĆö worth the early alarm. Bring layers.

activities.title: Japanese breakfast at the ryokan
activities.description: Grilled fish, miso soup, tamagoyaki, rice. Slow morning at the ryokan after Chureito sunrise.

activities.title: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway
activities.description: 3-min cable car to Mt Tenjo viewpoint.

activities.title: Nishiki Market
activities.description: Kyoto's kitchen ŌĆö pickles, tofu, skewers. Map opens at Aritsugu knife shop (mid-market anchor) ŌĆö Street View nearby stalls.

activities.title: Pontocho dinner
activities.description: Lantern alley along the Kamogawa. Map opens at Pontocho-Suishin (mid-alley) ŌĆö Street View to pick a restaurant.

activities.title: Hanamikoji, Gion
activities.description: Lantern-lit machiya street. Do NOT photograph geiko/maiko. Map opens at Ichiriki Chaya (iconic red ochaya corner).
activities.address: Ichiriki Chaya, 569 Gionmachi Minamigawa, Higashiyama, Kyoto

activities.title: % Arabica Higashiyama
activities.description: First stop together ŌĆö minimalist Kyoto coffee with view of Yasaka Pagoda. Leave the hotel ~8 AM, ~20-30 min by taxi/bus.

activities.title: Shirakawa geisha district
activities.description: Willow-lined canal. Map opens at Tatsumi Bridge ŌĆö iconic Shirakawa photo spot.
activities.address: Tatsumi Bridge, Gion Shirakawa, Higashiyama, Kyoto

activities.title: Inari Station
activities.description: Sunrise mission ŌĆö leave The Thousand Kyoto ~06:30. JR Nara line two stops from Kyoto Station.

activities.title: Fushimi Inari Taisha ┬Ę sunrise hike
activities.description: Timing is everything ŌĆö at 7 AM the 10K torii path is nearly empty, mystical light, photographers only. Hike to the Yotsutsuji intersection viewpoint (~45 min up, 30 min down). Skip the summit if tired ŌĆö Yotsutsuji is where the view opens. Free, 24/7.

activities.title: Saga-Arashiyama Station
activities.description: Back to Kyoto Station, switch to JR Sagano line out to Arashiyama (~45 min total from Fushimi).

activities.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
activities.description: Still beating the 10 AM tour bus rush. Walk through quickly ŌĆö atmosphere drops the busier it gets.

activities.title: Otagi Nenbutsuji (1200 stone faces)
activities.description: ~30 min walk uphill from bamboo grove. 1200+ rakan stone figures carved by amateur sculptors 1981ŌĆō91 ŌĆö each face is a different emotion: smiling, drinking, meditating, laughing, crying. Quiet, atmospheric, very photogenic.

activities.title: Adashino Nenbutsuji (8,000 Buddhas)
activities.description: 5 min walk back down from Otagi. Thousands of small weathered stone Buddhas memorializing the forgotten dead, plus a small bamboo grove behind the main hall. Less famous than the main Arashiyama grove ŌĆö but you'll have it almost to yourselves.

activities.title: Lunch in Arashiyama town
activities.description: Walk back down toward Togetsukyo. Yudofu (tofu hot pot) is the Arashiyama signature ŌĆö try Shoraian or Yudofu Sagano. Or grab matcha soba at Arashiyama Yoshimura with bridge views.

activities.title: Togetsukyo Bridge
activities.description: Crossing the Katsura river ŌĆö classic Arashiyama postcard. Short walk after lunch.

activities.title: Chushojima Station
activities.description: JR Sagano line back to Kyoto, transfer Keihan to Chushojima. ~45 min.

activities.title: Gekkeikan Okura Sake Museum
activities.description: Closes 4:30 PM ŌĆö tight but doable. Brief tour + tasting flight at the end. Edo-era brewery, the sake culture's birthplace neighborhood.

activities.title: Bee's Knees ┬Ę Kyoto speakeasy
activities.description: Last Kyoto nightcap. Quiet, tucked-away speakeasy, classic cocktails. Get dinner around Kawaramachi area first, then walk over. Cash preferred.

activities.title: Shinsaibashi arcade
activities.description: Covered shopping street running 600m. Map opens mid-arcade ŌĆö Street View to walk it.

activities.title: Hozenji Yokocho
activities.description: Moss-covered Buddha, tiny bars. Map opens at Hozen-ji Temple (moss Buddha).

activities.title: Koshien Station
activities.description: Hanshin Main Line from Umeda or Namba. ~30 min.

activities.title: Hanshin Koshien Stadium
activities.description: Tigers official store (brother's hat) + Koshien History Museum. Stadium tour available if pre-booked. Famous yakisoba shop across the street if hungry. Allow 2-3 hours.

activities.title: Janjan Yokocho
activities.description: Lantern alley. Map opens at the entrance arch.
activities.address: Janjan Yokocho arcade entrance, Naniwa, Osaka

activities.title: Bar Nayuta ┬Ę speakeasy
activities.description: Dotonbori-area speakeasy. Hidden door, classic + creative cocktails, intimate bar. Walk 15 min back from Shinsekai to Namba. Cocktails ┬ź1500ŌĆō2500. Cash preferred.

activities.title: Fuglen Tokyo
activities.description: Quiet Saturday-afternoon reset before Takarazuka. Norwegian roastery in a Scandinavian-modern Tomigaya space ŌĆö Reddit's go-to chill coffee spot near Yoyogi/Shibuya. ~10 min walk from TRUNK.

activities.title: Lunch in Roppongi Hills
activities.description: Roppongi Hills mall and the surrounding streets have everything ŌĆö Toraya for sweets, Tsurutontan for udon, Bricolage Bread & Co for casual.

activities.title: National Art Center
activities.description: Kurokawa's wavy glass facade ŌĆö the building itself is the attraction even if the rotating exhibit doesn't grab you. Free to wander the atrium + cone-shaped cafe inside.

activities.title: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
activities.description: Walk across Tokyo's iconic intersection ŌĆö 3,000 people per light cycle. Take the photo from the Starbucks above (Tsutaya 2F, free). 3 min walk to the Hachiko statue. 20-30 min total before going up Sky.

activities.title: Shibuya Sky
activities.description: Sunset rooftop ŌĆö you'll be looking DOWN at the Scramble you just walked. Pre-book the 5pm slot online.

activities.title: Bar High Five ┬Ę nightcap
activities.description: Hidetsugu Ueno's legendary Ginza bar ŌĆö 5 min walk from Hashimoto. Classic cocktails, no music, intimate counter. Reservation strongly recommended. ┬ź3-4K per cocktail. The full-stop on the best night of the trip.
activities.address: Efflore Ginza 5 Building B1, 5-4-15 Ginza, Chuo City

activities.title: Tsukiji Outer Market
activities.description: The original fish-market site (the auction moved to Toyosu but the outer market stayed). Best between 7-9 AM. Sushi-for-breakfast vibes, knife shops, tamago skewers, tuna nigiri at counter pace. Walking distance to Ginza for the rest of the day.
activities.address: Tsukiji Outer Market, 4 Chome Tsukiji, Chuo City

activities.title: Endo Sushi (Tsukiji)
activities.description: Sushi for breakfast ŌĆö the Tsukiji ritual. ┬ź3000-5000 pp set menus, sit at the counter, watch the chef work. Lines move fast 8-9 AM.

activities.title: Cat Street vintage cluster (Harajuku)
activities.description: Better fit-odds than Shimokita for tall/broader bodies ŌĆö these are US-sourced. Walk Cat Street + side alleys: 2nd Street (consistent sizing), BerBerJin (Western Levi's including bigger waists), Komehyo Harajuku (used designer, accurate sizing), Atlantis Vintage (US/EU vintage). Skip Kinji/Pin Nap (small Japanese-only sizing).

checklist.title: Kozantei Ubuya ┬Ę 3 nights (Nov 27ŌĆō29)
checklist.detail: Direct booking or Relux. Mention honeymoon.

checklist.title: Tokyo hotels ┬Ę 3 nights start + 3 nights end
checklist.detail: Same hotel both stays = easier baggage drop.

checklist.title: Omakase splurge ┬Ę Day 14 (Dec 7)
checklist.detail: Pocket Concierge / TableCheck. Options: Hashimoto, Tokami, Manten.

checklist.title: Camellia tea ceremony ┬Ę Day 8 (6 PM English)
Fuji Excursion train ┬Ę Day 4 (Nov 27)
checklist.detail: JR East ŌĆö Shinjuku ŌåÆ Kawaguchiko.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward ┬Ę TRUNK ŌåÆ Ubuya (Nov 27)
checklist.detail: Drop bags at TRUNK front desk Nov 26 evening or Nov 27 AM (~3 PM Yamato cutoff). ~┬ź2-3K per bag. Arrives at Kozantei Ubuya by 6 PM same/next day. Travel with small overnight bag for Fuji Excursion train + first ryokan night essentials.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward ┬Ę Ubuya ŌåÆ The Thousand Kyoto (Nov 30)
checklist.detail: Drop at Ubuya front desk Nov 29 evening. Saves you wrangling bags through the bus + 2 shinkansen transfers Mishima ŌåÆ Kyoto. ~┬ź2-3K per bag.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward ┬Ę Cross Osaka ŌåÆ TRUNK Tokyo (Dec 6)
checklist.detail: Drop at Cross Hotel front desk Dec 5 evening. Skip the 2.5-hr Nozomi luggage hassle + the post-2020 oversized-bag reservation requirement. ~┬ź2-3K per bag.

wishlist.title: My Only Fragrance ┬Ę custom perfume
wishlist.description: 90-min experience: smell ~20 scents, pick 4-5, they blend a custom perfume for you both, named. ~┬ź9,500 pp. Could fit Day 8 afternoon (squeeze before/after kimono) or Day 11 morning. Walk-in possible but reservations smarter.

wishlist.title: Ring engraving (Glanta or Ginto, Kyoto)
wishlist.description: Walk-in ring engraving ŌĆö ┬ź3-8K depending on length. Bring rings you already own + a word you collected on the trip. Plan: pick the word during Days 4-10, get engraved before leaving Kyoto Day 11.

wishlist.title: Bees Knees ┬Ę Kyoto speakeasy
wishlist.description: Tucked-away Kyoto speakeasy, classic cocktails, intimate. Alternative or backup to Yoramu sake (Day 7) if you want a cocktail night instead.

wishlist.title: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
wishlist.description: Iconic photo if you want a temple-heavy day. Skipped in your current plan to avoid temple fatigue, but you could squeeze in Day 11 morning before Osaka travel.
